narrative:
Lisandro Martínez is a Centre-Back for Argentina, playing a crucial role in their defensive setup. He holds an impressive overall rating of 7.65/10, which places him in the top 1% of centre-backs at this tournament. This rating is significantly influenced by his club form over the last two seasons, contributing 68% of the score.

At this World Cup, Martínez demonstrates strong defensive capabilities with 5.1 clearances per 90 minutes, complemented by 0.9 tackles and 0.6 interceptions per 90 minutes. Interestingly, his 0.3 goals per 90 minutes and 0.3 assists per 90 minutes far exceed his expected goals of 0.016 and expected assists of 0.204, respectively. This suggests highly clinical or opportunistic attacking contributions despite a low volume of shots and key passes.

Martínez serves as a vital defensive presence for his national team, securing the backline with consistent actions. Scouts and fans should watch for his continued high volume of defensive interventions, particularly his clearances, in remaining matches. It will also be key to observe if his unusually high goal and assist output, relative to his underlying attacking metrics, proves sustainable throughout the tournament.
